title: Outova Growth Creative System
summary: Campaign-ready creative, AI-assisted imagery, SEO-supportive content, and video production across multiple home and outdoor brands.

metrics.label: Portfolio examples named on the resume: Patio Productions, Starfire Design/Direct, Harmonia Living, Artificial Turf Supply
Web creative, social assets, blog support, GIFs, loops, and product videos

challenge: Support several portfolio brands at once without letting the work become generic, visually inconsistent, or too slow for campaign timelines.
approach: Created campaign-ready assets for web and social while adapting tone, styling, and product context to each brand.
Produced AI-assisted product and lifestyle imagery, then handled retouching, compositing, and visual cleanup in Photoshop.
Drafted and refreshed SEO blog content using AI writing tools with originality checks and quality control.
Built video assets in multiple ratios, including 16:9 and 9:16, plus product loops and GIFs for ongoing marketing use.
results: Expanded from static design work into a broader production role covering images, copy support, and motion assets.
Helped portfolio brands move faster with ready-to-use creative for paid, organic, and content channels.
Built flexible asset outputs that could be reused across social, site content, and editorial support.

sections.heading: What the work covered
paragraphs: This role moved beyond traditional graphic design. The work included campaign creative, product and lifestyle image generation, post-production, content drafting support, and video editing. The emphasis was not just making assets look polished, but making them usable across fast-moving eCommerce workflows.

Because the portfolio covered multiple brands, the challenge was to preserve each brand’s tone while still shipping quickly. That required structured visual decision-making, adaptable layouts, and reliable production habits.

sections.heading: Why it matters
paragraphs: For brands that run multiple campaigns and content streams at once, speed without quality control becomes a competitive advantage. This work shows the ability to operate as a marketing designer who can bridge design, production, content support, and asset readiness.
